data.description:
# Workflow engine: conditional overlays + delegating batch step

## Context

Split out of the workflow-engine remediation epic soupy-spool-revel (6f2d) per the brainstorm
in session log boom-demon-jug (ad3f) and a follow-on design session (this work). The e6d9
engine (declarative IR + thin worklist interpreter, run-state on the git event log; step kinds
`uses`/`prompt`/`branch`/`loop`/`map`, with bounded-concurrent `map` fan-out) is closed. To
express a reusable 4-pass LLM-review pipeline ON the engine (epic B migrates plan-review +
completion-verification onto it), the engine needs exactly TWO new concepts. This epic adds
ONLY those concepts to the engine — no plan-review coupling. A prior-art survey (Argo, AWS Step
Functions, dbt, LangGraph, Airflow/Dagster, Temporal) grounded the split below.

## Two concepts (the split is decisive)

1. **Conditional overlay = a first-class DECLARATIVE construct.** Conditional step *inclusion*
   is declarative in every engine that has it (Argo `when:`, dbt `config(enabled=…)`, Step
   Functions `Choice`). A prompt step gains a `when`/`enabled` predicate evaluated over
   RECORDED inputs (ticket material, has-linked-session-log, has-children). The RESOLVED set is
   journaled at run-start so replay reads the record (never recomputed against a possibly-mutated
   store). Low risk, high value; it is also the artifact a visual editor adds-to / removes-from.

2. **Batch orchestration = a THIN delegating step, NOT first-class control flow.** No surveyed
   engine makes 'adaptive cost-budgeted batching with runtime fallback + model-escalation' a
   first-class primitive — it is app code everywhere — and encoding it in the IR would break the
   interpreter's replay invariant (batch composition depends on a runtime token estimate + which
   call hit a context limit, so its control state is a recorded DECISION-TREE, not a function of
   recorded outputs). So the IR gains a thin `batch` step declaring only parameters
   (`{group, usd_budget, model_ladder}`); a batch-RUNNER (behind a seam) does the adaptive
   packing / token-estimation / context-limit fallback / model-escalation / shed-to-INDETERMINATE
   / fingerprint-checkpoint and JOURNALS its batch-plan + fallback transitions as opaque events
   the interpreter stores but never interprets. The thin interpreter and the Burr-tripwire
   discipline are preserved. (Epic B supplies the PRODUCTION batch-runner by extracting the gate's
   sizing.py behind this seam; epic A ships the seam + a reference/default batching implementation.)

## Scope (engine-only)

- The `when`/`enabled` overlay predicate: schema + JSON Schema + interpreter resolution +
  run-start journaling of the resolved set + validation + dry-render support.
- The `batch` step kind: schema/JSON Schema + the batch-runner SEAM (protocol) + a reference
  default batch-runner + the journaled batch-plan/fallback event vocabulary + interpreter wiring
  (store the opaque plan, do not interpret it) + validation.
- A THROWAWAY demo workflow exercising BOTH constructs end-to-end (dry-run via FakeAgentRunner),
  removed by epic B after the migration; retained meanwhile as the visual-editing refinement sample.
- VISUAL EDITING for the two NEW components only (the overlay predicate + the batch step) in the
  workflow editor — bounded to the new constructs, not a general canvas.

## Success Criteria

- The engine expresses conditional criterion inclusion and budgeted batch orchestration as
  first-class concepts, WITHOUT plan-review coupling and WITHOUT breaking the replay-determinism
  / thin-interpreter (Burr-tripwire) invariants.
- A demo workflow proves both constructs offline; the new constructs are visually editable.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `when`/`enabled` overlay predicate on a prompt step is parsed, schema-validated, and
      resolved by the interpreter over recorded inputs; the resolved included-set is JOURNALED at
      run-start and read from the record on replay (a replay test proves a mid-run store edit does
      not change the resolved set).
- [ ] A `batch` step kind exists: declarative params (`group`, `usd_budget`, `model_ladder`); a
      batch-runner SEAM + a reference default runner; the runner journals its batch-plan + fallback
      transitions as opaque events the interpreter STORES but does not interpret (a test asserts the
      interpreter never branches on batch internals).
- [ ] Replay determinism holds for both constructs (resolved overlay-set + batch-plan are recorded,
      not recomputed); the Burr-tripwire stays green (no asyncio/threading leaks outside the
      confined fan-out).
- [ ] A throwaway demo workflow exercises overlay + batch end-to-end under the FakeAgentRunner
      (offline, no live calls), with a test; it is clearly labeled disposable.
- [ ] The workflow visual editor can render/add/remove the overlay predicate and the batch step.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.
data.title: Workflow engine: first-class conditional overlays + delegating batch step (engine-only; + throwaway demo + visual editing)
